prerequisites: A Google account with one or more Blogger blogs you want to read.
A project in the Google API Console (Google Cloud Console) with the Blogger API v3.0 enabled.
An OAuth 2.0 client credential (client ID and client secret) created in that project.
An OAuth 2.0 refresh token granted for the scope https://www.googleapis.com/auth/blogger.
The configuration values client_id, client_secret, and client_refresh_token, all of which are required.

authMethods.name: OAuth 2.0 (refresh token)
authMethods.summary: The Blogger API v3.0 authorizes requests for user data with OAuth 2.0. This connector uses the three OAuth credentials from its descriptor: client_id and client_secret identify your Google API Console application, and client_refresh_token is exchanged for short-lived access tokens. Authorize the scope https://www.googleapis.com/auth/blogger when generating the refresh token.

setupSteps.title: Create a Google API Console project and enable the Blogger API
setupSteps.body: In the Google API Console (Google Cloud Console), create or select a project and enable the Blogger API v3.0 for it. This is required before requests will be served.

setupSteps.title: Create OAuth 2.0 client credentials
setupSteps.body: In the project's Credentials section, create an OAuth 2.0 client ID. Note the generated client ID and client secret; these become the connector's client_id and client_secret values.

setupSteps.title: Authorize and obtain a refresh token
setupSteps.body: Run the OAuth 2.0 authorization flow requesting the scope https://www.googleapis.com/auth/blogger and grant consent with the Google account that owns the target blogs. Capture the refresh token returned by the flow; this becomes the connector's client_refresh_token.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector
setupSteps.body: Provide the required fields client_id, client_secret, and client_refresh_token. The connector exchanges the refresh token for access tokens to call the Blogger API.

sources.title: Blogger API v3.0 Authentication and authorization
Using OAuth 2.0 to Access Google APIs
